title: Sesi Built-in Functions Reference
description: A comprehensive guide to the core built-in functions, standard library modules, and system operations available in the Sesi programming language.
summary: Sesi provides a robust set of built-in functions for I/O, type conversion, collection manipulation, file system interaction, networking, and system-level tasks. It supports structured error handling, module imports via a hierarchical resolution path, and includes specialized functions for workflow orchestration and tool definition.

code_examples.code:
define_tool("summarizer", fn(t) { return "Summary: " + t }, "Summarizes text")
let result = workflow([{"prompt": "Summarize:"}], "Input text")

import { PI, sqrt } from "std/math"
import { sleep } from "std/time"

key_rules: Paths for file system functions are resolved relative to the current working directory.
The `workflow` function automatically chains step outputs as input for subsequent steps.
Module resolution follows a strict priority: Script directory > Current working directory > SESI_PATH > ~/.sesi/lib.
Structured error handling is performed via `try/catch` blocks; `raise_error` terminates execution.
Collection functions like `push` modify arrays in-place.
Standard library modules (v1.3+) must be imported using the `import { ... } from "std/..."` syntax.
Functions like `num`, `len`, `keys`, and `values` return `null` upon failure or invalid input.
